original: Former peasant Justin I proclaimed Byzantine emperor in the Hippodrome, Constantinople
Origin of Armenian calendar
The city of Dublin is founded on the banks of the river Liffey
The most severe of several early fires of London burns in Southwark, south of London Bridge
English "Good Parliament" ends, longest-sitting parliament at that time
Battle on Eems: Dutch water garrison defeats Spanish
Coronation of John III, King of Sweden
Spanish army leader Richebourg conquers Liefkenshoek, Belgium
Spanish theater plays "Moros y Los Cristianos" in Rio Grande
Catholic German monarchy forms the Catholic League
First non-Separatist Congregational Church in US founded in Salem, Massachusetts
England declares war on The Netherlands - beginning of the 1st Anglo-Dutch war
Nine Years' War: Battle of Beachy Head - French fleet under Tourville defeats Anglo-Dutch fleet under Cornelis Evertsen in the English Channel near East Sussex [O.S. 30 June]
The British Indian Government establishes the Fort William College to promote Urdu, Hindi, and other vernaculars of the subcontinent
The Vellore Mutiny is the first instance of a mutiny by Indian sepoys against the British East India Company
President Jackson vetoes legislation to recharter the Second Bank of the US
Lincoln writes to Kentucky's militia and says Union troops will not enter that state
Battle of Charleston, South Carolina, Morris Island [->Sep 06]
Battle of Jackson, Mississippi - captured by Federals [->Jul 16]
Indelible pencil patented by Edson P. Clark, Northampton, Massachusetts
The then villa of Mayagüez, Puerto Rico, formally receives its city charter from the Royal Crown of Spain
First day of Test Cricket at Old Trafford, Manchester (1st Test, England v Australia) is washed out
George Goldie gets charter for Royal Niger Company
Wyoming becomes 44th state of US, the first with female suffrage
Jean-Baptiste Marchand's expedition reaches Fashoda at White Nile
H Kamerlingh Onnes makes helium liquid (-269°C)
105°F (41°C) at North Bridgton, Maine (state record)
Hannes Kolehmainen runs world record 5000 m (14:36.6)
Romania declares war on Bulgaria
World's official highest recorded temperature of 134°F (56.7°C) occurs at Greenland Ranch (now known as Furnace Creek Ranch) in Death Valley, California
British South African troops march into German South-West Africa
Russian Soviet Federal Socialist Republic is formed
Dutch parliament approves women's right to vote
All non-fascist parties dissolved in Italy
Two-pound hailstones kill 23 people and many cattle in Rostov, Russia
Denmark takes Greenland as Norway ends claim
Railroad worker strike ends in Amsterdam
Meher Baba begins his silence of 44 years. His followers still observe Silence Day on this date in commemoration.
USSR's official news agency TASS is formed
Lake Denmark, New Jersey, arsenal explodes, killing 21 and causing $75 million in damage
Senator Milt Gaston hurls record-tying 14-hit shutout
Pittsburgh Pirates outslug Philadelphia Phillies 15-9 at Baker Bowl; 9 HRs hit, 1 in each inning - unique in MLB history
US issues newer, smaller-sized paper currency
Jack Burnett gets 9 hits, Eddie Rommel relieves in second 18-17 victory in 18 innings as his A's beat Indians in longest relief job
First police radio system operated in Eastchester Township, NY
109°F (43°C) Cumberland and Frederick, Maryland (state record)
110°F (43°C) at Runyon, New Jersey (state record)
111°F (44°C) Phoenixville, Pennsylvania (state record)
112°F (44°C) at Martinsburg, West Virginia (state record)
New Straits Convention allows Turkish re-armament of Dardanelles
"Yankee Clipper" completes first passenger flight over Atlantic
Continuation War: Finland invades East Karelia as hostilities with the Soviet Union resume following the German invasion in June
Jedwabne Pogrom: massacre of Jewish people living in and near the village of Jedwabne, Poland
Netherlands government in exile in London recognizes Soviet Union
Sixth day of Battle of Kursk, USSR: Operation Citadel continues
US, British, and Canadian forces invade Sicily in WWII (Operation Husky)
German submarine U-821 sunk by RAF
Admiral Marc Mitscher named chief of US Navy staff
Belgian government of Acker resigns
200 die when train derails and falls into a river in Canton, China
Cleveland Indian Don Black no-hits Philadelphia A's 3-0
Lydda Airfield captured by Israeli army
First practical rectangular TV tube announced in Toledo, Ohio
WJAR TV Channel 10 in Providence, Rhode Island (NBC) begins broadcasting
"Your Hit Parade" premieres on NBC-TV (later CBS) after being broadcast on radio since 1935
Armistice talks to end Korean conflict begin in Kaesong
650,000 US steel workers go on strike
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Atoll
Ex-king Norodom Sihanoek appointed premier of Cambodia
First parking meters installed in England (625 installed)
Belgium sends troops to Congo
Telstar, first active communications satellite developed by American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T), is launched
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
Jesús Alou is the first San Francisco Giant in 40 years to get six hits in a game in a 10-3 win over the Chicago Cubs at Wrigley Field
Moïse Tshombé, leader of the Confederation of Tribal Associations of Katanga, becomes Prime Minister of the Congo
The Beatles release "A Hard Day's Night", their third studio album
Beatles' "VI" album goes to number 1 and stays at number 1 for 6 weeks
Rolling Stones score their first US number 1 single "(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction"
Bobbie Gentry records "Ode to Billie Joe," which goes on to win four Grammy awards
Uruguay becomes a member of the Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works
US Major League Baseball announces it will be split into two divisions for 1969
Chilean Association of Librarians created
Democratic Convention opens in Miami Beach, Florida (McGovern)
Herd of stampeding elephants kills 24 in Chandaka Forest, India
Bahamas declares independence from the United Kingdom and adopts constitution
John Paul Getty III, grandson of oil tycoon J. Paul Getty, is kidnapped in Rome by Italian gangsters demanding a ransom
OPEC ends oil boycott against Netherlands
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeastern Kazakhstan
Chemical factory in Seveso, near Milan, explodes covering the surrounding area in dioxin
One American and three British mercenaries are executed in Angola following the Luanda Trial
Bloodless military coup in Mauritania; President Moktar flees
Alexandra Palace in London burnt down for a second time
Sam Shepard's play "True West" premieres at the Magic Theatre in San Francisco
Willie Jones hospitalized for heatstroke from record 46.5°C temperature
CERN achieves first proton-antiproton beam collision (570 GeV)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Walt Disney's "Fox & The Hound" released
Miguel Vasquez makes first public quadruple somersault on trapeze
Rangers' Larry Parrish hits his third grand slam of the week

Prolific studio drummer Jim Gordon is convicted of murdering his mother and sentenced to 16 years in prison. Diagnosed with schizophrenia after the killing, he is serving time in a medical/psychiatric prison and has been denied parole 10 times as of 2018.
Coca-Cola Co announces it will resume selling old-formula Coke
Paula Ivan runs female world record 1 mile (4:15.61)
Foreign Minister R.F. Botha of South Africa signs accession to the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty on behalf of South Africa
L'Express Airlines Beechcraft C-99 crashes in Alabama, killing 13
Spaceship Giotto (Halley 1986) approaches comet Grigg-Skjellerup
US Major Soccer League folds after 14 seasons
Melchior Ndadaye becomes first Hutu Burundi President, and Sylvie Kinigi becomes Prime Minister
Yobes Ondieki runs world record 10 km (26:58.38)
Nepal Prime Minister Girija Prasadkoirala resigns
Sonia O'Sullivan runs female world record 2 km (5:25.36)
Hideki Irabu makes MLB debut as a NY Yankee, beats Tigers 10-3
RJR Nabisco announces it will replace Joe Camel in new ads
Spain, Partido Popular member Miguel Ángel Blanco is kidnapped in the Basque city of Ermua by ETA members, sparking widespread protests
Roman Catholic sexual abuse cases: The Diocese of Dallas agrees to pay $23.4 million to nine former altar boys who claimed they were sexually abused by former priest Rudolph Kos
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Rose Bowl, Pasadena, CA: US beats China 5-4 on penalties; 0-0 a.e.t; US wins second World Cup
A leaking southern Nigerian petroleum pipeline explodes, killing about 250 villagers scavenging gasoline
EADS, the world's second-largest aerospace group, is formed by the merger of Aérospatiale-Matra, DASA, and CASA
Amerada Hess agrees to acquire Triton Energy for $2.7 billion in cash
At a Sotheby's auction, Peter Paul Rubens' painting The Massacre of the Innocents is sold for £49.5 million (US$76.2 million) to Kenneth, Lord Thomson

Neoplan bus owned by Kowloon Motor Bus collides with a truck, falls off a bridge on Tuen Mun Road, Hong Kong, and plunges into the underlying valley, killing 21 people, making it the deadliest traffic accident in Hong Kong
Hurricane Dennis slams into the Florida Panhandle causing billions of dollars in damage
Pakistan International Flight PK-688 crashes in Multan, Pakistan shortly after takeoff, killing all 45 people on board
Former Macedonian Interior Minister Ljube Boškoski is acquitted of all charges by a UN Tribunal accusing him of war crimes
British tabloid News of the World publishes its last edition after 168 years in the wake of a phone hacking scandal
The American Episcopal Church becomes the first to approve a rite for blessing gay marriages
40 people are buried in landslides in Sichuan Province, China
Yair Lapid warns of Israeli Defense Force ground operation if Gazan rocket fire do not stop
The Confederate flag is taken down for the last time from South Carolina Capitol grounds 1 day after the state legislature ordered its removal
Twenty-three people are killed and fifty are injured in a stampede at a free clothing drive in Mymensingh, Bangladesh
NASA's Juno spacecraft makes closest-ever pass over Jupiter's Great Red Spot at 9,000 kilometers overhead
Original sketch of Winnie the Pooh's Hundred Acre Wood by E.H. Shepard sells for £430,000 in London, a record price for a book illustration
The final four boys and their coach are rescued from Tham Luang Nang Non cave, Thailand, after being trapped there for 18 days by monsoon flooding
British ambassador to the US Sir Kim Darroch resigns after his secret cables calling the US president "inept" were published
Earliest evidence of modern humans outside Africa is found with 210,000-year-old skull from Apidima Cave, southern Greece, published in "Nature"
More than 61,000 people die in European heatwaves in 2022, according to study published in "Nature Medicine", suggesting world not doing enough to counter increasing heatwaves
Torrential rains across New England and New York cause historic flooding, especially in Vermont's capital Montpellier, affecting two million people and causing one death

The original "Birkin bag," designed for singer and fashion icon Jane Birkin in 1984 by French design firm Hermès, becomes the most expensive handbag ever, selling at a Sotheby's auction for €8.6 M ($10M), with fees; purchaser later revealed to be Japanese CEO Shinsuke Sakimoto
Battle of Dyrrhachium:Julius Caesarbarely avoids a catastrophic defeat toPompeynear the city of Dyrrachium (in what is now Albania)
Wars of the Roses: Richard of York defeats KingHenry VIat the Battle of Northampton; Henry VI is captured
KingCharles VFrance and KingHenry VIIIof England sign Treaty of Calais
LadyJane Grey, daughter of the Duke of Suffolk, is proclaimed Queen of England, succeedingEdward VI, who declares his half-sisters illegitimate, and reigns for 9 days
English fleet underGeorge Villiersreaches La Rochelle [NS = June 20]
Battle at Langport, Somerset:Oliver Cromwell's New Model Army defeats Royalists
KingGeorge IIauthorizes the Admiralty Board to seek maritime reprisals against Spain (War of Jenkin's Ear)
Scotland'sBonnie Prince Charlieflees in disguise to Isle of Skye
Louis-François Roubiliac's monument toGeorge Frideric Handelis unveiled at Westminster Abbey in London
Horatio Gatesissues order excluding Blacks from Continental Army
TheComte de Rochambeauand his French force of 7,000 land in Newport, Rhode Island, to join the American Revolutionary War
Carl Friedrich Gaussdiscovers that every positive integer is representable as a sum of at most three triangular numbers
Urbain Le Verrier and John Couch Adams, co-discoverers of Neptune, meet for first time at home ofJohn Herschel
Millard Fillmoreis sworn in as President of US, replacing Taylor
US begins construction ofCentral Pacific Railroad
French poet Paul Verlaine (29), in a drunken, jealous rage, wounds protégéArthur Rimbaud(18) with pistol and gets sentenced to 2 years in jail
DutchQueen Wilhelminaopens Technical Hague court
Emma Goldmanimprisoned for obstructing draft
Cleveland's future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielderTris Speakerhas his then-record hitting streak of 11 stopped by Tom Zachary; Indians beat Washington Senators 8-4 at Griffith Stadium
Jury selection takes place in USJohn T. Scopesevolution trial
Franklin D. Rooseveltvisits Colombia, becoming the first sitting US president to visit South America
Phillies'Chuck Kleinbecomes fourth player to hit four home runs in a game
Belgian-Romani-French jazz guitaristDjango Reinhardt's "Quintette du Hot Club" debuts at La Grosse Pomme nightclub in Montmartre, Paris
Howard Hughesflies around the world in 91 hours
Himmlerorders sterilization of all Jewish women in Ravensbrück concentration camp in northern Germany
"Father of Medicare"Tommy Douglasbecomes the seventh Premier of Saskatchewan
Muhammad AliJinnahis recommended as the first Governor General of Pakistan by then Prime Minister of the United Kingdom,Clement Attlee
Martin Luther King Jr.arrested during demonstration in Georgia
100th British Open Men's Golf, Royal Birkdale:Lee Trevinowins the first of his consecutive Open Championships, a stroke ahead of Lu Liang-Huan of Taiwan
Failed assassination attempt on KingHassan IIof Morocco kills 101
National Women's Political Caucus (NWPC) founded in US by women, including Bella Abzug,Betty Friedan,Shirley Chisholm, Myrlie Evers-Williams, andGloria Steinem
Gladys Knight& the Pips Summer Series premieres on NBC-TV
Test cricket debut ofGraham Goochagainst Australia, out for a pair
World News Tonight premieres on ABC withMax Robinsonas the first Black anchor on a network newscast in the US
Chuck Berrybegins 4-month prison term for $200,000 in tax evasion
"Escape from New York," directed byJohn Carpenterand starringKurt Russelland Donald Pleasence, premieres in the US
Samuel Morse's "Gallery of the Louvre" sells for $3,250,000
Playboy and Penthouse publish nude pictures of pop singer-songwriterMadonna
Andrew Dice Claycries onArsenio HallShow
Boris Yeltsinis sworn in as the first elected President of the Russian Federation
Louise Woodwards' trial begins in Massachusetts nanny murder trial
Coldplayrelease their debut album "Parachutes" (Grammy Award Best Alternative Album 2002)
10th ESPY Awards:Tiger WoodsandVenus Williamswin
Jason Koumasannounces his retirement from professional football
Drakesurpasses The Beatles' record of most singles in the Billboard Hot 100 with seven from his album "Scorpion" against their five
Real Madrid forwardCristiano Ronaldojoins Italian champions Juventus in a deal worth £99.2 million, becoming one of the four most expensive players of all time
Kawhi Leonardsigns a reported 3-year, $103 million contract with the Los Angeles Clippers
Taylor Swiftnamed the world's highest paid entertainer by Forbes earning $185 million in 2018
Fourteenth-century document by a civil servant asking for time off identified as only known handwriting byGeoffrey Chaucer, the "Father of English Literature"
US PresidentJoe Bidenvisits the UK, meeting KingCharles IIIat Windsor Castle and Prime MinisterRishi Sunakin Downing Street
Lady Godiva rides naked on horseback through Coventry, according to legend, to persuade her husband, the Earl of Mercia, to lower taxes
US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonpersonally delivers Treaty of Versailles to Senate
Battle of Britain begins as Nazi forces attack shipping convoys in the English Channel

French foreign intelligence agents blow up the Greenpeace flagship Rainbow Warrior in Auckland harbor, New Zealand, to prevent it from interfering with French nuclear tests in the South Pacific. Dutch photographer Fernando Pereira is killed.
Sixth-century cathedral Hagia Sophia is turned into a mosque by decree issued by Turkish President Recep TayyipErdogan(converted to a mosque 1453-1934)
Go-Hanazono, 102nd Emperor of Japan (1428-64), born in Japan (d. 1471)
James III, King of Scots (1460-88), born in Stirling or St Andrews Castles (d. 1488)
